name: Ladysmith Federal Savings And Loan Association
description_short: Ladysmith Federal is a mutually-owned community bank in Wisconsin established in 1934, offering full-service banking including checking, savings, loans, and credit cards with a focus on local service.
description_long:
Ladysmith Federal Savings and Loan Association was founded in 1934 as a small savings and loan association with a single staff member focused on mortgages and savings products. Over nine decades, it has evolved into a thriving, full-service financial institution with 12 staff members and an active board of directors. The bank maintains its original mission while expanding to meet modern customer needs.

The bank offers a comprehensive range of retail banking services including checking accounts, savings accounts, certificates of deposit, debit cards, credit cards with cash-back rewards, share loans, free online bill pay, and mobile banking. Their in-house credit card program allows customers to earn cash back on everyday purchases with flexible reward options tailored to personal or business needs. All accounts can be managed through online banking and mobile apps for convenient access.

Ladysmith Federal distinguishes itself as the only locally-owned bank in Rusk County, Wisconsin, operating as a mutual institution where customers are owners. The bank emphasizes community involvement and investment, participating in Wisconsin Bankers Association initiatives like Power of Community Week, offering document shredding services, organizing park cleanups, and visiting local schools to teach financial literacy to elementary students. This grassroots approach and mutual ownership structure differentiate it from larger regional and national competitors.

As a small community bank, Ladysmith Federal may have limited product complexity and geographic reach compared to larger institutions. However, for customers prioritizing local relationships, mutual ownership benefits, and community reinvestment, the bank delivers personalized service from familiar staff members. The emphasis on community engagement and locally-controlled decision-making appeals to customers seeking banking alternatives to large corporate institutions. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
pros: Mutually-owned institution where customers are owners, not shareholders
Only locally-owned bank in Rusk County, Wisconsin
12-person staff focused on personal relationships and expert service
In-house credit card with customizable cash-back rewards
Free online bill pay and mobile banking included
Active community involvement including financial literacy programs for K-2 students
92-year history of stability and local presence since 1934
cons: Small institution may offer fewer product options than larger regional or national banks
Limited geographic reach restricted to Rusk County area
Website does not display current interest rates, fees, or account minimum requirements
No mention of specialized products like investment services or wealth management
Smaller customer base may result in less competitive rates on some products
best_for: Rusk County, Wisconsin residents seeking local banking relationships and community reinvestment
Customers who prefer mutual ownership and locally-controlled decision-making over corporate structures
Small business owners wanting personalized banking with flexible credit card options
Community-minded depositors prioritizing banks active in local schools and civic engagement
diagnosis: Ladysmith Federal is best for Wisconsin residents in Rusk County seeking traditional community banking with personalized service, mutual ownership benefits, and strong local community involvement. The main caveat is that as a small institution, it offers limited product breadth and geographic convenience compared to larger banks, making it most suitable for those prioritizing local relationships over extensive service offerings or nationwide branch networks.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
meta_description: Ladysmith Federal is a Wisconsin community bank established in 1934, offering checking, savings, loans, and credit cards with a focus on local service.
fdic_official_name: Ladysmith Federal Savings and Loan Association
